plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for a stronger income component.
Add to SPY, targeting 35% for broad market stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single stock concentration.
Reduce SMH to 10% to lower semiconductor sector risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown potential but also limiting upside.

plan: Sell SOXL entirely to remove high leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% for minimal leveraged exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth.
Increase SMH to 15% to lean into quality semiconductor exposure.
Buy XLK at 5% for diversified technology sector exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single stock concentration.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for roughly 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still seeking growth, accepting moderate drawdowns.

plan: Add to SOXL, targeting 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductors.
Increase SMH to 20% for concentrated semiconductor exposure.
Add to CRWD, targeting 15% for high-growth AI software.
Buy MRVL at 5% and ALAB at 5% based on strong scanner setups.
Trim QQQI to 10% and SPY to 25% to fund higher-conviction growth plays.
Maintain strict stops on all positions, especially leveraged ETFs.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It significantly increases leverage and concentration in high-beta tech, accepting much larger potential drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all SOXL, SPAL, and CRWD to remove high-beta and leveraged exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to further reduce sector-specific risk.
Add to QQQI, targeting 40% for primary income generation.
Increase SPY to 35% for broad market stability and some growth.
This plan prioritizes monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions, but remember distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is income-focused, not a savings account.
